code_map.current_responsibilities: Dispatch CLI commands and compose WorkerConfig, PullwiseClient, and the single ReviewWorkerV1 instance.
code_map.boundary: Lifecycle and readiness commands are imported lazily; the run path owns only composition.
code_map.candidate_extraction_seam: Keep this as the narrow composition root while legacy command implementations remain in their current modules.

code_map.current_responsibilities: Parse instance configuration, isolate provider paths, collect host metrics, build strict-v1 registration and heartbeat HTTP requests, and normalize control-plane errors.
code_map.boundary: Owns transport/configuration mechanics; review semantics remain in review_worker_v1.py.
code_map.candidate_extraction_seam: Separate host/provider configuration and metrics from the Pullwise HTTP client and strict-v1 payload builders.

code_map.current_responsibilities: Evaluate provider, plan, runtime, path, disk, and Codex readiness and expose doctor and device-login flows.
code_map.boundary: Provides operator-facing doctor and device-login readiness results; the live daemon independently derives provider_ready from Codex SDK health and quota before WorkerState.can_lease().
code_map.candidate_extraction_seam: Separate plan/provider validation, Codex readiness probing, and operator-facing doctor rendering.

code_map.current_responsibilities: Run service actions and log streaming, supervise lifecycle commands, update the Worker and Codex CLI, and perform contained instance cleanup.
code_map.boundary: Provides host service, log, update, uninstall, and cleanup operations; review job state and phase dispatch remain in review_worker_v1.py.
code_map.candidate_extraction_seam: Split log streaming, watcher command state, staged update/install, destructive instance cleanup, and idle cache pruning.

code_map.current_responsibilities: Maintain the one active job slot, register/heartbeat/lease loop, cancellation supervisor, current job lifecycle, and phase dispatch.
code_map.boundary: The Server owns the global queue and outer lease; this module owns one in-process active slot and no local queue.
code_map.candidate_extraction_seam: Extract active-marker recovery, control-loop transport, cancellation supervision, and phase orchestration behind narrow state interfaces.

code_map.current_responsibilities: Contain worker-scoped Codex runtime resources, event scopes, token usage, bounded SDK calls, approval denial, and model-turn workspace publication.
code_map.boundary: The SDK owns App Server lifecycle; Worker code owns containment, fencing, bounded waits, and declared output publication.
code_map.candidate_extraction_seam: Move remaining SDK client/quota and model-turn publication responsibilities out of review_worker_v1.py into runtime-focused modules.

code_map.current_responsibilities: Use the 30-entry PIPELINE_PHASES registry to order progress and dispatch semantic and mechanical work; prepare phase-specific prompts, fan out domain reviewers, and derive bounded intent execution capabilities.
code_map.boundary: prepare_workspace executes before its no-op registry branch; terminal submission executes after submit_result_envelope is marked complete; active-slot cleanup runs in run_job's finally path. Codex produces semantic turn outputs, while Worker code validates and publishes declared outputs and enforces ordering, limits, and artifact contracts.
code_map.candidate_extraction_seam: Extract phase registry/dispatch, prompt construction, fanout, bundle planning, and intent execution into cohesive one-way modules.

code_map.current_responsibilities: Freeze repository inventory, protect disposable validation source, validate evidence/report bindings, run legacy QA, and materialize/upload versioned artifacts.
code_map.boundary: Current inventory and QA are legacy full-scan facts; this map does not assign future SourceState, Gate, or Agent Kernel ownership.
code_map.candidate_extraction_seam: Separate source inventory/integrity, intent workspace validation, QA/report validation, and artifact manifest/upload responsibilities.

code_map.current_responsibilities: Persist active-run identity, recover unfinished publication state, journal immutable terminal result payloads, replay retryable submissions, and record cancellation supersession.
code_map.boundary: This is the sole current terminal-result outbox path; it recovers publication transactions, not mid-pipeline semantic execution.
code_map.candidate_extraction_seam: Extract active-slot persistence, terminal outbox validation/replay, and cancellation supersession into a durable publication module.

code_map.current_responsibilities: Build the current Worker debug bundle and independently audit bundle structure, counters, finding/evidence bindings, and redaction boundaries.
code_map.boundary: Debug evidence excludes source-bearing bundles and is distinct from audit download and future cross-end Assembly semantics.
code_map.candidate_extraction_seam: Separate bundle input loading, rule groups, issue aggregation, and CLI/report rendering; keep builder and independent auditor distinct.

code_map.current_responsibilities: Estimate whole-scan remaining time from the current run dependency graph and publish sanitized progress snapshots.
code_map.boundary: Uses current-run monotonic observations only and does not own phase semantics or cross-run forecasting.
code_map.candidate_extraction_seam: Keep the estimator independent; move only orchestration-specific work-unit wiring behind an adapter if the phase registry is extracted.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Strict-v1 daemon, active slot, Codex runtime bridge, fixed review pipeline, integrity/QA, artifacts, terminal outbox, debug, and checkout utilities.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Control loop and active slot; runtime/quota; phase registry and executors; intent validation; evidence/QA; artifacts/result/debug; checkout and storage utilities.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Legacy broad contract and behavior coverage for review_worker_v1.py.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Move cases by contract or failure mode into focused active-slot, pipeline, intent, artifact, terminal-publication, and checkout suites.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Service/log commands, lifecycle watcher, staged updates, uninstall, contained cleanup, and idle cache/log pruning.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Log streaming; watcher state; update/install; uninstall containment; idle workspace/cache/log cleanup.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Agentic execution, intent source materialization, workspace integrity, preflight, repair, and evidence contract coverage.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Split capability discovery, source materialization, integrity, preflight, execution, repair, and result-contract cases.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Load debug bundle evidence, execute cross-artifact audit rules, aggregate issues, and render machine/Markdown reports.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Bundle loading; lifecycle/progress checks; finding/evidence checks; intent checks; report rendering and CLI.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Host/provider setup, safe file helpers, metrics, Worker configuration, strict-v1 payload builders, HTTP client, and uninstall entry compatibility.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Host dependencies/provider environment; safe IO/metrics; configuration; protocol payloads; HTTP transport/errors.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Terminal-result outbox replay, cancellation supersession and conflict handling, durable active markers and upload manifests, pre-claim recovery and readiness, post-receipt cleanup ordering, idle workspace and mirror cleanup, and uninstall containment regressions.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Split terminal outbox, supersession, conflict, and durability cases from active-marker recovery/readiness and workspace, mirror, and uninstall cleanup cases.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Codex SDK timeout, cancellation, event scope, runtime health, shutdown, and archive regressions.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Split bounded RPC/runtime health, event scopes, turn lifecycle, archive, and shutdown failure suites.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Absolute deadline, cancellation supervision, child process termination, and terminal publication race regressions.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Split deadline propagation, cancellation state/events, subprocess termination, and terminal race cases.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Debug bundle directory and ZIP audit coverage across identity, artifacts, progress, findings, validation and intent evidence coherence, size limits, and insufficient-evidence rejection.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Split bundle loading and size-limit cases from progress/artifact, finding/validation, intent-evidence, and insufficient-evidence cases.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Bundle resource limits and intent subprocess, repair, executable preflight, and approved-snapshot binding regressions.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: The existing test classes are direct seams for separate bundle-limit, subprocess, repair, executable, and binding files.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Thread-cumulative Codex token attribution and run/phase usage ledger regressions.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Keep cohesive unless phase aggregation and concurrent-thread attribution need independent suites.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Provider/plan/host readiness evaluation plus doctor and Codex login presentation.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Plan/provider validation, readiness probes, and operator presentation are the natural seams if growth is required.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Reviewer plan-cap rejection, low-memory concurrency reduction, ETA critical-path and live-scheduling behavior, fatal sibling cancellation, thread-archive failure, and one transient-capacity retry with concurrency reduction.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Separate scheduling and ETA behavior from fatal cancellation/archive and transient-capacity retry behavior if the suite grows.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Semantic grouping validation, exact coverage, bounded rendering/splitting, assignments, and plan resource limits.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Separate grouping contract, renderer/splitting, assignment derivation, and resource-limit suites if growth is required.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: CLI dispatch and host lifecycle/readiness/update/uninstall command contracts.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Split CLI parser/dispatch from lifecycle, update, uninstall, and doctor command tests if growth is required.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Dependency scheduling, resource concurrency, retry rewiring, confidence, deadline, and sanitized ETA snapshot tests.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Keep estimator tests cohesive; split scheduling from snapshot/confidence behavior only if growth is required.

file_baselines.current_responsibilities: Symlink-safe SDK log IO, event/turn usage scoping, runtime resource health/close, bounded calls, and identifier validation.
file_baselines.candidate_extraction_seam: Keep the runtime resource aggregate cohesive; safe IO and token/event accounting are the first seams if new duties are added.
